13:12 - Critical alert: Port-side gun deck ports are now submerged. Water ingress reported in the hold.

13:15 - Massive shift in balance; ship heeling rapidly to 15 degrees. Order given to move all personnel to the starboard side.

13:18 - Emergency: List increasing to 25 degrees. Impossible to recover; structural integrity failing.

13:20 - Total loss of telemetry. Ship sunk.

description: A batch job which scans for unused blocks in Amazon S3 and marks them to be freed encountered a condition where all retries for freeing certain blocks would fail. The batch job logs its actions to local disk and this log grew without bound. When the filesystem filled, this caused other filesystem writes to fail, and the Tarsnap service stopped. Manually removing the log file restored service.
raw_input:
10:15 UTC - PagerDuty alert: Service Tarsnap reporting 5xx errors; elevated latency on API requests.

10:22 - Confirmed service down. Checking service logs—receiving "No space left on device" errors across multiple processes.

10:30 - Investigating storage nodes. /var/log/ shows 100% disk usage on primary partition.

10:45 - Attempting to identify large files. 'du -sh /var/log/*' indicates a massive file associated with the S3 cleanup batch job.

10:55 - Batch job process is hung. Disk remains full, blocking any new log writes or cache operations for core service.

11:05 - Killing batch job process. Manually truncating the oversized log file to reclaim disk space.

11:12 - Storage cleared. Restarting Tarsnap service processes.

11:20 - Service heartbeat returning to normal. Monitoring disk growth rates for the next hour.

description: A fire in a datacenter caused SMS text messages to be sent to random destinations. Corrupt messages were also experienced by customers.
raw_input:
09:12 UTC - PagerDuty alert: Spike in 5xx errors on SMS gateway API. Latency climbing above 5s.
09:18 UTC - Customer Support reporting high volume of tickets regarding "gibberish" or corrupted SMS content.
09:24 UTC - Multiple reports of users receiving SMS messages they did not initiate; some users reporting messages sent to wrong recipients.
09:35 UTC - Datacenter connectivity alerts firing for Region US-EAST-1. Paging SRE lead and Network Engineering.
09:42 UTC - Outbound message queue depth at 400% of capacity. Clearing queue is failing due to timeout errors.
09:55 UTC - Total loss of heartbeat from primary messaging cluster in US-EAST-1. Declaring SEV-1.
10:10 UTC - Initiating emergency failover to US-WEST-2; observing inconsistent message delivery state across regional DB sync.
10:25 UTC - Traffic successfully diverted to secondary region. Corrupt message reports subsiding, but message logs indicate unauthorized delivery attempts persist.

description: The Therac-25 was a radiation therapy machine involved in at least six accidents between 1985 and 1987 in which patients were given massive overdoses of radiation. Because of concurrent programming errors, it sometimes gave its patients radiation doses that were thousands of times greater than normal, resulting in death or serious injury.
raw_input:
08:15 - Patient treatment session interrupted. Console reporting "MALFUNCTION 54" error code. System locked up.

08:18 - Attempted system reset. Operator reports patient complained of a "burning sensation" during the brief activation window.

08:25 - Investigating "MALFUNCTION 54" in technical manual. Definition is ambiguous—appears to be a transient hardware sensor trip.

08:40 - Hardware technicians cleared the machine; no physical damage found. Restarting unit for diagnostic calibration.

09:05 - Second treatment session, same patient. Console flashed "MALFUNCTION 54" again during beam delivery. 

09:12 - Patient reports acute skin pain. Immediately suspending all clinical use of this unit. 

09:30 - Escalating to engineering team. Error logs are inconsistent; the software interface seems to report "dose delivered" despite the hardware abort signal.

09:55 - Initiating full system diagnostic lock-out. We cannot explain the discrepancy between commanded beam and sensor readout. Requesting urgent audit of control software.

description: Due to a human error, all engineers lost access to the central source code management platform (GitHub organization). An Azure Active Directory Security group controls the access to the GitHub organization. This group was removed during the execution of a manual and repetitive task.
raw_input:
09:12 UTC - PagerDuty alert triggered: "High rate of 403 Forbidden errors on github.internal.corp".

09:15 UTC - Multiple reports on Slack #eng-help: engineers unable to clone repos, CI/CD pipelines failing across all projects.

09:18 UTC - Verifying access; personal GitHub tokens are returning 404/403. Cannot access organization dashboard.

09:22 UTC - Checking Azure AD portal. The "GitHub-Developers" security group is missing from the assigned enterprise application list.

09:25 UTC - Incident declared: SEV-1. Paging IAM and DevOps leads.

09:30 UTC - Confirming that all organization membership is effectively wiped. DevOps team checking audit logs for recent group modifications.

09:45 UTC - Still locked out. Attempting to restore via group re-assignment, but changes are stuck in Azure sync delay.

10:05 UTC - Sync delay cleared. Access restored for users. Investigating who triggered the group membership change.

description: In 2013, a temporary network partition in the redis cluster used for billing operations, caused a massive resynchronization from slaves. The overloaded master crashed and when it was restarted, it started up in read-only mode. The auto-recharge component in This resulted in failed transactions from Twilio's auto-recharge service, which unfortunately billed the customers before updating their balance internally. So the auto-recharge system continued to retry the transaction again and again, resulting in multiple charges to customer's credit cards.
raw_input:
14:02 UTC - Alert: Billing-service-prod latency spike detected. Error rate climbing > 15%.
14:05 UTC - Users reporting multiple credit card charge notifications on Twitter/Support tickets.
14:08 UTC - Redis cluster alert: Master node unresponsive. Observed massive spikes in inbound traffic/resync logs.
14:12 UTC - Automated billing jobs failing with "Read-only file system/mode" errors. 
14:15 UTC - Incident declared. Rolling restart of Redis master cluster initiated.
14:20 UTC - Redis master back up, but billing-service logs show persistent transaction failures. Retries surging.
14:25 UTC - Confirmed: Auto-recharge component is looping. Billing transactions are succeeding at the gateway but failing to update local balance.
14:30 UTC - Paging Billing team lead. Need to halt auto-recharge worker queues immediately.

description: Twilio's incident of having high filtering on SMS towards AT&T Network In United States.
raw_input:
14:12 UTC - PagerDuty alert: Spike in 300xx delivery errors for US-based SMS traffic.
14:15 UTC - Dashboard shows 85% delivery failure rate specifically for carrier AT&T; other carriers unaffected.
14:22 UTC - Eng responding. Confirming no recent deployment or config changes to outbound routing engine.
14:30 UTC - Validated connectivity with primary US carrier partners; upstream gateways reporting high volumes of traffic, but AT&T endpoints returning "Blocked" responses.
14:45 UTC - Opened SEV1 ticket with upstream transit partners to investigate potential rate-limiting or filtering at the carrier ingress point.
15:05 UTC - Initiated traffic shaping to throttle outbound volume towards AT&T to prevent further reputation flagging.
15:30 UTC - Symptoms persist; customer support reporting high volume of tickets regarding undelivered alerts and MFA codes.
16:10 UTC - On-call lead escalating to Carrier Relations team for direct contact with AT&T operations.

description: Steam's desktop client deleted all local files and directories. The thing I find most interesting about this is that, after this blew up on social media, there were widespread reports that this was reported to Valve months earlier. But Valve doesn't triage most bugs, resulting in an extremely long time-to-mitigate, despite having multiple bug reports on this issue.
raw_input:
14:10 UTC - High volume of reports incoming on community forums; users claiming Steam client is deleting local game files and folders upon launch.

14:18 UTC - Internal support dashboard flagging anomalous ticket spike related to "file deletion" and "disk wipe."

14:25 UTC - Engineering alerted. Initial triage shows pathing logic errors in the client update routine. Cannot reproduce locally yet.

14:35 UTC - Panic in Slack. Multiple senior engineers confirming they have received user reports mentioning the client is purging its own install directory.

14:42 UTC - Decision made to pull the latest client update from the CDN to prevent further impact.

14:50 UTC - Public relations team requesting statement on "Steam deleting user data." Engineering still trying to isolate the specific logic bug.

15:05 UTC - Searching internal bug tracker history. Finding references to similar file system behavior reported months ago; tagging ticket as "Critical" and escalating to core client team.

description: The website for Xubuntu, a derivative of Ubuntu Linux that uses Xfce as its desktop environment, was compromised by an attacker who changed download links to a malicious zip file.
raw_input:
14:12 UTC - Automated monitoring alert: Web server integrity check failed. Unexpected file hash detected for xubuntu-22.04-desktop-amd64.iso.

14:15 - Multiple reports coming in via IRC and Twitter: Users claiming the downloaded ISOs are triggering antivirus warnings or failing checksum verification.

14:19 - PagerDuty triggered: Critical alert for elevated 404/403 errors on the primary download mirror index page.

14:24 - On-call engineer logged into web server. Observed unauthorized modifications to index.html and redirect scripts in the document root.

14:31 - Confirmed: Links for the main desktop release are pointing to an external domain hosting a suspicious .zip file instead of the official .iso.

14:38 - Initiating emergency site lockout. Disabling write access to the web server and flushing the CDN cache to prevent further distribution of malicious payloads.

14:45 - Investigating potential breach vector. Access logs show unusual SSH activity from an unfamiliar IP address at 13:55 UTC. Escalating to security team.

description: A network partition in a cluster caused some messages to get delayed, up to 6-7 hours. For reasons that aren't clear, a rolling restart of the cluster healed the partition. There's some suspicious that it was due to cached routes, but there wasn't enough logging information to tell for sure.
raw_input:
09:12 UTC - PagerDuty alert: High latency on message processing queue. p99 spike from 50ms to 4s.

09:25 UTC - Slack channel activity: Engineering reporting reports of "stale data" and UI hanging for multiple customers.

09:45 UTC - Monitoring dashboard shows queue depth growing exponentially. Consumer nodes appear healthy but throughput is near zero.

10:15 UTC - Incident triggered. Investigating potential upstream dependency failure. Database metrics look stable.

12:30 UTC - No progress. Confirmed messages are being acknowledged but are stuck in an "in-flight" state for hours. 

14:05 UTC - Decision made to initiate a rolling restart of the cluster nodes as a last-resort mitigation.

14:20 UTC - Rolling restart in progress. Queue depth beginning to drop rapidly. Messages from earlier this morning are finally flushing.

14:45 UTC - Latency returned to baseline. Traffic flow normalized. Resolving incident; will need to investigate why nodes were holding onto delayed packets.

description: The Order Management System (OMS) provided to Zerodha, a stock broker, collapsed when an order for 1M units of a penny stock was divided into more than 0.1M individual trades against the typical few hundreds, triggering a collapse of the OMS, which was not encountered prior by its provider - Refinitiv (formerly Thomson Reuters), a subsidiary of the London Stock Exchange.
raw_input:
09:12 UTC - Alerts firing: High latency on OMS gateway. Increasing timeout errors on trade execution endpoint.
09:15 - OMS message queue depth spiking rapidly. Currently at 50k backlog.
09:18 - Multiple customer support tickets coming in: "Orders stuck in pending state" and "Cannot cancel existing orders."
09:22 - PagerDuty triggered: Critical threshold exceeded for OMS CPU utilization. Service is unresponsive to heartbeats.
09:27 - Escalated to Refinitiv support. OMS service fully hung. DB connection pool exhausted.
09:35 - Manual restart of OMS primary node attempted; service crashing immediately upon startup. 
09:42 - Investigating specific large-volume order ID: [REDACTED]. System appears to be choking on high volume of micro-fills.
10:05 - OMS remains down. Coordinating with vendor engineering teams on emergency patch.

description: A failure of the primary leased line to a CTCL between a stock broker and a stock exchange led to the activation of a backup leased line that was operating sporadically over the following hour, affecting bracket and cover orders. Subsequently, the process of placing and validating orders had been modified to incorporate the unreliability of the CTCL's leased lines, but the reliability of the primary and the backup leased lines was not fundamentally improved by the providers.
raw_input:
09:12 UTC - Alerts firing: High latency on primary CTCL connection. Trading engine reporting order timeouts.

09:15 UTC - Client reports failing to execute bracket orders. Monitoring shows primary link down; traffic failing over to secondary leased line.

09:22 UTC - Secondary link unstable. Intermittent 503 errors and "Connection Reset" logs spiking in the exchange gateway module.

09:40 UTC - PagerDuty escalation: Significant increase in stale order validations. Manual intervention required to clear message queues.

10:05 UTC - Secondary link flapping. Ping loss oscillating between 15% and 40%. Connectivity to exchange remains erratic.

10:35 UTC - Orders continuing to hang in "Pending" status. Decided to implement rate-limiting and increased retry jitter to accommodate link instability.

11:00 UTC - Order flow stabilized, though latency remains elevated. Monitoring secondary line for persistent packet loss.
